name: ground_truth_ir4_incident_handling_ambiguous
description: Ground-truth fixture modeled on the Example System IATT assessment pattern for IR-4 incident handling. Simulates a scenario where an incident response plan exists but has not been exercised — the assessor cannot confirm the implementation matches the documented procedures without test results. LLM signals abstain directly with confidence 0.2. Tests the confidence-floor abstain gate with an IR-family control where implementation evidence is characteristically harder to obtain on an IATT (no incidents have occurred yet).

ccis_row.guidance: 000822: The organization implements an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
ccis_row.procedures: The organization conducting the inspection/assessment obtains and examines the incident response plan and interviews organizational personnel to ensure the organization implements an incident handling capability.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- Incident Response Plan (draft) — defines preparation, detection/analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery phases but marked as draft pending tabletop exercise.
- Interview notes — security team confirmed plan exists but no incidents have been handled under the IATT.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined incident response plan; confirmed via the documented IRP that preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery phases are defined. Tabletop exercise records were unavailable for review under the current IATT, so implementation-vs-procedure conformance could not be independently corroborated.
